review_cadence: review before every release-readiness run and after any Service v2 gate promotion

stages.label: Design-partner alpha
stages.intent: Controlled, named design-partner pilots over the current single-node semantic kernel and packaged pilot service.
stages.claim_boundary: AETHER can be sold or shown as a paid, closely supported pilot for exact single-node semantic coordination. It is not a generalized distributed runtime or managed SaaS.

gates.title: Core v1 semantic closure is defensible
Service v2 boundary is visible but scoped

evidence.label: HTTP namespace and Postgres integration tests

next_actions: Keep SQLite as the packaged default.
Present Postgres as optional journal-first infrastructure only.

gates.title: Hardening promotion state is explicit
blockers: Admin and operator hardening packs are not yet promoted to blocking release gates.
next_actions: Accumulate the required scheduled green streaks.
Promote admin first, then operator, while keeping user and exec diagnostic until stable.

stages.label: Commercial beta
stages.intent: Paid commercial beta for selected customers with a repeatable deployment contract, explicit support boundaries, and blocking operational evidence.
stages.claim_boundary: AETHER can be sold as a supported single-node or Service v2 deployment for bounded production-adjacent workflows. Distributed truth remains prototype-grade unless explicitly scoped.

gates.title: Service v2 operational proof is blocking
next_actions: Keep release-readiness -CommercialBetaCandidate green after package build.
Keep Postgres and container evidence blocking in CI, with live local reruns when customer deployment context requires them.
Do not promote user or exec hardening packs until their own release-blocking criteria are explicitly accepted.

gates.title: Backup, restore, and upgrade contract is customer-grade
next_actions: Keep the generated rollback record attached to every beta-candidate release-readiness run.
Use the Service v2 proof artifact as the package-local SQLite backup/restore/restart evidence.
Keep Postgres export/restore documented as external database-operator responsibility until a live customer deployment requires a stronger drill.

gates.title: Security and key lifecycle are beta-ready
next_actions: Keep the package token-rotation drill, token-command tests, auth reload test, and SBOM/checksum manifest green in release-readiness.
Treat SBOM/checksum as commercial-beta evidence only; signed artifact provenance remains a GA gate.
Validate customer-specific external secret-manager commands during deployment onboarding without changing policy semantics.

gates.title: Performance trends support beta commitments
next_actions: Keep core and service drift non-failing on the canonical Windows host.
Keep restart/replay, report, and pilot HTTP read-path latencies under the tracked beta ceilings.
Keep replicated-partition measurements observational until variance is understood.

gates.title: One design-partner workflow is packaged end to end
next_actions: Keep the Demo 05 customer workflow acceptance artifact attached to release-readiness output.
Promote full notebook execution from structural smoke to blocking only after the Colab runtime path is stable enough for CI.
Add customer-specific evidence appendices per design partner without changing the canonical workflow gate.

stages.label: General availability
stages.intent: Broad commercial release with repeatable artifact promotion, support posture, security posture, and conservative public claims.
stages.claim_boundary: AETHER can be sold beyond named design partners with supported deployment, upgrade, security, observability, and incident-response commitments. Distributed runtime claims still require separately gated evidence.

gates.title: Signed artifact and promotion pipeline
blockers: Release-readiness is still a pre-release verification flow, not a signed artifact promotion pipeline.
No GA-grade versioned changelog, artifact signing, or provenance attestation is enforced.
next_actions: Add signed artifact generation and verification.
Tie release promotion to a versioned changelog and immutable release evidence bundle.

gates.title: Support, incident, and disclosure posture
blockers: Responsible disclosure exists, but public bug bounty and customer support commitments are not yet in force.
Incident response and escalation playbooks are not yet formalized for external customers.
next_actions: Create customer incident-response and escalation playbooks.
Decide if and when to launch a paid public bug bounty.

gates.title: Multi-platform distribution is supported
blockers: Linux/macOS package parity and installation verification are not yet GA gates.
next_actions: Add Linux and macOS package builders or explicitly scope GA to one supported platform.
Publish install and rollback evidence per supported platform.

gates.title: Distributed truth claims are separately gated
blockers: Replicated authority partitions are a single-host manual-failover prototype.
No automatic election, quorum consensus, multi-host failover, or replicated sidecar plane is implemented.
next_actions: Keep distributed runtime language out of GA claims until separately proven.
Continue exact-provenance partition work without pulling Raft or broad consensus into the beta gate.
